brief_justification: Paper A is geometric/string-theoretic mirror symmetry: hyperkähler intermediate Jacobians, 3d Rozansky-Witten theories, and an SYZ transform exchanging A- and B-branes on Calabi-Yau threefolds. Paper B is a stochastic-process problem: periodic threshold removal (harvesting) steering the evolution of probability densities to quasi-steady states with an effective drift. The two share no transferable machinery -- one is derived-category/SYZ duality on hyperkähler geometry, the other is truncation operators acting on Fokker-Planck-type density dynamics -- so no role-for-role mapping holds beyond the incidental word 'mirror'.
